scope: L group round three postmatch model backtest only: Croatia vs Ghana; Panama vs England. No betting advice.

actual_result.note: Croatia one-goal win; result direction aligned with model, exact score just outside Top5 but close to median one-goal-win cluster.

market_calibration_error.ordinary_1x2_calibration_read: Market was stronger than model on Croatia home win; actual direction supports market, while red-team discount was directionally conservative.
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: handicap draw because Croatia won by exactly one goal
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: The exact one-goal win bucket was the correct settlement layer; it must not be replaced by ordinary Croatia win probability.

postmatch_error.summary: Croatia win direction hit, but 2-1 was just outside Top5 and over 2.5 beat the low-total lean.

factor_error.market_signal: Market home-win lean helped; red-team discount correctly kept uncertainty but may have reduced Croatia direction too far.
factor_error.tactical_signal: Croatia midfield/control and set-piece/second-phase pressure were enough for the one-goal win, while Ghana still retained one-goal transition/set-piece path.
factor_error.player_state_signal: Croatia veteran control and Budimir/Kramaric-style penalty-box routes were slightly underweighted in the 2-1 branch.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 2-1 was not Top5 but carried about 8.1% mass and ranked near the main cluster. This is not a heavy-tail miss; it is a one-goal win plus BTTS/over-2.5 branch that should be more visible.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: Draw floor was not the primary miss. The model already lifted draw after red-team, but actual match confirmed Croatia edge.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: Third-round must-win pressure for Croatia was handled directionally, but the model kept the total-goal profile too low once Ghana had to chase and Croatia could still find a second.

actual_result.note: England win and 0-2 exact score hit the Top2 cluster; strong-team late break existed but did not become a blowout.

market_calibration_error.ordinary_1x2_calibration_read: Ordinary 1X2 was missing in the captured packet; England direction is evaluated by model only.
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: handicap draw because England won by exactly two goals
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: The +2 draw bucket matched the final margin; the model had the exact two-goal corridor visible through 0-2 as Top2.

postmatch_error.summary: England win direction hit and 0-2 was Top2. The model captured a strong-team controlled win without forcing a 3+ blowout.

factor_error.market_signal: Ordinary 1X2 market was missing, but +2 handicap draw aligned with the exact two-goal corridor.
factor_error.tactical_signal: England low-block breaking improved enough for 0-2, but Panama defensive resistance still prevented the blowout tail.
factor_error.player_state_signal: England quality and bench/late-break edge were present, while rotation/minutes management kept the ceiling below a large win.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 0-2 was the second-most likely score and hit Top3/Top5. No score-tail miss; the lesson is to keep the two-goal favorite corridor separate from 3+ blowout.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: Draw floor was reasonably discounted; Panama 0-goal attack and England pressure removed the draw path.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: Third-round context was mostly calibrated: England still pushed for group position and found two goals, but rotation/low-block resistance kept the match from becoming a big-margin tail.

common_diagnosis.direction_layer: Both primary 1X2 directions hit after red-team probabilities: Croatia home win and England away win.
common_diagnosis.score_layer: Panama-England exact score hit Top2; Croatia-Ghana missed Top5 but 2-1 was near the central one-goal-win cluster, not an extreme tail.
common_diagnosis.totals_layer: Croatia-Ghana under-2.5 lean missed because BTTS plus Croatia second goal arrived; Panama-England under profile was well calibrated at exactly two total goals.
common_diagnosis.market_layer: Croatia ordinary 1X2 market helped home-win direction; both handicap matches landed on exact-margin handicap draw buckets, underscoring the need to separate WDL from handicap settlement.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.02 to +0.04 into 2-1/2-0/1-goal-win branches when Croatia must win and fields Budimir/Kramaric/Modric or similar set-piece/control spine.
parameter_updates.cap: do not add more than +0.03 to home win directly; mostly redistribute score matrix.
parameter_updates.reason: Croatia 2-1 Ghana was directionally correct but the BTTS one-goal-win score sat just outside Top5.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.03 to +0.05 into 0-2/1-3/2-goal-margin branch for elite favorite versus low block when favorite still needs group position but may manage minutes.
parameter_updates.cap: keep 3+ blowout tail separate; do not automatically lift blowout probability.
parameter_updates.reason: England 0-2 Panama shows late/controlled break without a large-margin explosion.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: cap under-2.5 confidence below 0.62 when favorite/control team must win and opponent has credible transition or set-piece one-goal route.
parameter_updates.cap: confidence cap only unless xG total is also revised.
parameter_updates.reason: Croatia-Ghana over 2.5 emerged from a 2-1 branch despite low-total baseline.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: always publish exact one-goal/two-goal margin bucket beside ordinary 1X2 in third-round reports.
parameter_updates.cap: schema/reporting change; no direct probability shift.
parameter_updates.reason: Croatia -1 draw and Panama +2 draw were both the actual handicap settlement layer.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: when market and model both prefer favorite and must-win context is strong, limit red-team favorite discount to -0.02 to -0.03 unless lineup/availability shock is confirmed.
parameter_updates.reason: Croatia home-win discount was directionally conservative but actual supported the favorite.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: keep Panama/low-creation underdog scoring floor suppressed unless official lineup shows multiple transition starters; no upward change after 0-2.
parameter_updates.cap: do not set BTTS floor above 0.32 in similar low-creation profile.
parameter_updates.reason: Panama again failed to score; England clean-sheet path was correctly visible.

player_state_backtest_link.note: The user requested model backtest output only; data-thread player-state normalization is not assumed complete here.

quality_notes: No betting advice is included; odds are used only as calibration and settlement-layer diagnostics.
Actual scores were supplied by the main thread.
Output is ASCII-only to avoid encoding artifacts in this workspace shell.
